name: quimb (Quantum Information and Many-Body Tensor Networks)

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy/opt_einsum backend; GPU via cuTensorNet; contraction order critical

agent_readiness.notes: Quantum information and tensor network library. import quimb as qu. import quimb.tensor as qtn. Quantum states: qu.rand_ket(2**n). Hamiltonians: qu.ham_heis(n, sparse=True). Eigenvalues: qu.eigh(H). MPS: psi = qtn.MPS_rand_state(n, bond_dim=10). psi.H @ psi. DMRG: dmrg = qtn.DMRG2(H, bond_dims=[10, 20, 50]). dmrg.solve(). TEBD time evolution. Contract: qtn.TensorNetwork.contract(). Quantum circuit simulation. Tensor contraction order optimization via cotengra.

gotchas: Extremely domain-specific — quantum information and tensor networks only
Tensor contraction order dramatically affects performance — cotengra needed
DMRG requires understanding of MPS, bond dimensions, and DMRG algorithm
Documentation is dense and assumes physics background
best_when: Tensor network simulations (MPS, PEPS, MERA) of quantum systems
DMRG ground state calculations for 1D quantum chains
Quantum circuit simulation via tensor contraction
avoid_when: Without tensor network / quantum physics background
summary: quimb provides quantum information and tensor network tools for Python. MPS_rand_state(n, bond_dim=10). DMRG2(H).solve(). TensorNetwork.contract(). Quantum circuit simulation. Entanglement entropy, fidelity. cotengra for contraction ordering. Apache-2.0 by Johnnie Gray. Best for tensor network methods (MPS, DMRG) and quantum circuit simulation via contractions. Very domain-specific — requires quantum physics background.
